### 1. Apple Foundation Models — Claude integration documentation

Source facts:
- Anthropic published official docs for Apple Foundation Models integration with Claude, enabling hybrid cloud and on-device AI on Apple platforms.

Builder/operator takeaway: Major platform integration between Claude and Apple's on-device models. Directly relevant to anyone building AI tools on Mac/iOS, including MCP/agent workflows.

Uncertainty: Content was title-only in the fetch; full scope of integration features unclear without reading the docs.

### 2. Why AI hasn't replaced software engineers, and won't

- Source: [simonwillison.net](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/14/why-ai-hasnt-replaced-software-engineers/#atom-everything)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-06-16T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- In NY's first year of AI-related WARN Act disclosure requirements, zero companies reported AI-caused layoffs, suggesting AI is not causing mass software engineering job losses.

Builder/operator takeaway: Credible, data-backed analysis from respected researchers that counters common AI anxiety narrative with real numbers.

Uncertainty: WARN Act data only covers large layoffs (100+ employees) and may not capture smaller reductions.

### 3. Curl will not accept vulnerability reports during July 2026

- Source: [daniel.haxx.se](https://daniel.haxx.se/blog/2026/06/15/curl-summer-of-bliss/)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-16T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: vendor_claim, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- The curl project will not accept vulnerability reports during July 2026.

Builder/operator takeaway: Curl is ubiquitous infrastructure. A month-long vulnerability reporting freeze is unusual and worth knowing about for anyone who depends on curl.

Uncertainty: Unclear if this applies to critical/zero-day issues.

### 4. The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE

- Source: [planetscale.com](https://planetscale.com/blog/the-only-scalable-delete)
- Category: Data Infrastructure / Verification / Scraping
- Published: 2026-06-16T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: production_proven, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Large-scale DELETEs in Postgres are fundamentally unscalable; the recommended pattern is partition-based table rotation with DROP TABLE.

Builder/operator takeaway: Practical, well-explained guidance for a common Postgres scaling issue. Directly relevant to anyone running Postgres with growing tables.

Uncertainty: PlanetScale has a MySQL product interest, but the Postgres analysis appears technically sound.

### 5. Show HN: Kage — Shadow any website to a single binary for offline viewing

- Source: [github.com](https://github.com/tamnd/kage)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-06-16T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_private

- Kage is an open-source tool that bundles any website into a single binary for offline viewing.

Uncertainty: Unclear how well it handles dynamic/JS-heavy sites.
